title: Game Window — tkinter Setup
description: Create the main game window using tkinter. Build a GameWindow class that sets up a fixed-size window with a Canvas widget for rendering the dungeon map and a Label at the bottom for status messages. Use grid geometry manager for layout.
starter_code:
# GUI Dungeon Crawler — Step 1: Game Window
# Create the main tkinter window with Canvas and status bar.

import tkinter as tk

class GameWindow:
    """Main game window with a canvas for the map and a status label.

    Attributes:
        root: The Tk root window.
        canvas: A Canvas widget for drawing the dungeon map.
        status_label: A Label widget for status messages.
        width: Window width in pixels.
        height: Window height in pixels.
    """

    def __init__(self, title='Dungeon Crawler', width=800, height=600):
        """Initialize the game window.

        Args:
            title: Window title string.
            width: Window width in pixels.
            height: Window height in pixels.
        """
        self.width = width
        self.height = height
        # Your code here:
        # 1. Create the Tk root window and store as self.root
        # 2. Set the window title
        # 3. Make the window non-resizable (both directions)
        # 4. Call self.configure_grid() to set up layout
        # 5. Create self.canvas — a Canvas widget in row 0, col 0
        #    with a black background and size (width x height-40)
        # 6. Create self.status_label — a Label in row 1, col 0
        #    with white text on dark gray background
        # 7. Set initial status text to 'Welcome to the Dungeon!'
        pass

    def configure_grid(self):
        """Configure grid weights so canvas expands to fill space."""
        # Your code here:
        # Make row 0 (canvas) expand, row 1 (status) fixed
        # Make column 0 expand
        pass

    def set_status(self, text):
        """Update the status label text."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def run(self):
        """Start the tkinter main loop."""
        self.root.mainloop()

# Test it
if __name__ == '__main__':
    game = GameWindow()
    game.set_status('Use WASD or arrow keys to move.')
    game.run()
test_code:
game = GameWindow(title='Test Dungeon', width=800, height=600)
assert hasattr(game, 'root'), 'GameWindow must have a root attribute'
assert hasattr(game, 'canvas'), 'GameWindow must have a canvas attribute'
assert hasattr(game, 'status_label'), 'GameWindow must have a status_label attribute'
assert isinstance(game.canvas, tk.Canvas), 'canvas must be a tk.Canvas instance'
assert isinstance(game.status_label, tk.Label), 'status_label must be a tk.Label instance'
assert game.width == 800, 'width should be 800'
assert game.height == 600, 'height should be 600'
assert game.root.title() == 'Test Dungeon', "Window title should be 'Test Dungeon'"
game.set_status('Testing')
assert game.status_label.cget('text') == 'Testing', 'set_status should update label text'
game.root.destroy()
acceptance_criteria: GameWindow class creates a Tk root window with the given title
Canvas widget fills the main area with a black background
Status Label sits at the bottom with white-on-dark-gray text
configure_grid() sets up row/column weights for proper expansion
hints: Use grid(row=..., col=..., sticky='nsew') for layout
